title: Ridgemont ER: Pre-Escalation Bay Triage
strategies: single-bay FCFS baseline: use only bay 0 and ignore every other bay, serving the longest-waiting arrived patient with no preemption and no acuity awareness at all -- minimal effort, no parallelism, no urgency reasoning (the evaluator's calibrated trivial reference)

chase-the-sickest reactive greedy: every step, point every bay at the currently highest-acuity waiting patient, preempting whenever a waiting patient now looks worse than whoever a bay is treating -- reacts to crises instead of preventing them, and repeatedly pays the switch penalty plus setup downtime chasing flashy newcomers while quieter fast-climbers get ignored

predictive time-to-tip scheduling: use each waiting patient's own public rate/acuity to compute (C - acuity) / rate as a deterministic deadline, fill idle bays by soonest-to-tip first, and almost never preempt a running treatment (only a bounded, rare sacrifice to save a patient about to hit the death cap with no bay free) -- treats mid-acuity patients before they escalate instead of after

capacity-budgeted lookahead: with the full seeded arrival/rate schedule visible up front, solve a small assignment/matching problem per congestion window to decide who each bay should serve before it, further tightening the schedule beyond simple soonest-to-tip ranking
